title: Lasthenia coronaria
taxonomy: Lasthenia coronaria is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is also known as the golden rayless coreopsis. Lasthenia coronaria is classified as an annual or biennial herb, typically growing to a height of 10-20 inches. The plant produces small, yellow or white flowers that bloom in the spring and early summer.
uses: Lasthenia coronaria, also known as lewisia or goldenfields, has been used in traditional medicine by Native American communities. The plant contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any plant-based remedies. Research has shown that Lasthenia coronaria may have benefits in wound healing and skin conditions. Further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects in human health.
benefits: Lasthenia coronaria is also known as the Goldfields or Desert Gold, and it has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. properties: Lasthenia coronaria, also known as the Golden Ray or Golden Yarrow, has been found to possess potential therapeutic properties due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been traditionally used by indigenous communities in North America for its medicinal purposes, including treating various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. constituents: Lasthenia coronaria contains various active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The sesquiterpene lactones, in particular, have been shown to exhibit cytotoxic activity, making them of interest for potential medicinal applications. Additionally, Lasthenia coronaria has been found to contain phenolic acids and other polyphenolic compounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and mechanisms of action associated with these active constituents.
parts: Lasthenia coronaria, also known as lepidium or golden-rayed tarweed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: Lasthenia coronaria, also known as lepidium or goldenrays, is a plant used in herbal preparations due to its potential medicinal properties. intro: Lasthenia coronaria, also known as the goldenfields, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasthenia coronaria include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Lasthenia coronaria is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in tinctures, salves, and topical creams to help soothe and heal skin irritations and wounds.
